summary: The bundled reproduction cloned the vulnerable FacturaScripts commit, staged two proof files, started the application under Docker, and confirmed both a `/Core/Assets/*` path breakout and a `/MyFiles/Public/*` token-bypass read.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should read a repo-root JSON proof file through `/Core/Assets/../../evohunt-secret.json` and a private `MyFiles` JSON proof file through `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/2026/06/123.json` without any `myft` token.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `3d97ab4acf5037979fc653ef5e32f4e853a468f4`, staged the proof files, built the reproduction image, and emitted `CORE_ASSETS_BREAKOUT_OK`, `MYFILES_TOKEN_BYPASS_OK`, and `EVOHUNT_PROOF_OK` before exiting successfully.
verification_method: The container helper copies the pinned repository into a temporary workspace, restores the bundled `vendor/` tree, launches the PHP built-in server, and issues the two malicious HTTP requests with `CURLOPT_PATH_AS_IS`. Verification passes only when both responses are HTTP 200 and exactly match the staged proof strings.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run emitted `CORE_ASSETS_BREAKOUT_OK` after reading the repo-root proof file through `/Core/Assets/../../evohunt-secret.json`.
The run emitted `MYFILES_TOKEN_BYPASS_OK` after reading the private `MyFiles/2026/06/123.json` proof file through `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/2026/06/123.json` without a token.
The run emitted the final success marker `EVOHUNT_PROOF_OK` and `./reproduction/run.sh` exited with status 0.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, writes the minimal config and proof files into that checkout, and mounts it read-only into the container at /target-repo.
